summary: OCB TITAN ERP P1 Integration Testing - Iteration 49. Backend: 15/15 tests passed (100%). Frontend: Owner Edit Button NOT visible in PO list due to race condition bug.

ui_bugs.issue: Owner Edit Button does NOT appear in Purchase Orders table AKSI column, even for logged-in owner user
ui_bugs.root_cause: Race condition: isOwner(user) returns false because user is null when component first renders. AuthContext fetches user async from /api/auth/me but table renders before user data arrives.

P1_A_DISCOUNT_ENGINE.calculate_pricing: PASS - POST /api/master/calculate-pricing returns items, totals, discounts

P1_B_PRICE_LEVEL.price_for_customer: PASS - GET /api/master/price-for-customer/{product_id}/{customer_id} returns correct price level
P1_B_PRICE_LEVEL.price_levels_info: PASS - GET /api/master/price-levels returns 5 price level definitions

P1_C_OWNER_EDIT_API.owner_edit_po: PASS - PUT /api/owner/edit/purchase-order/{id} works for owner role

OWNER_ACCESS.full_access_verified: PASS - Owner role has access to all 4/4 tested endpoints

action_items: FIX HIGH PRIORITY: Owner Edit Button not visible in PurchaseModule.jsx
Root cause: isOwner(user) check at line 692 returns false because user is null during initial render

Solution options: 1) Wait for user to load before rendering table, OR 2) Remove outer isOwner check and rely only on OwnerEditButton internal check, OR 3) Add loading state check that waits for auth context to resolve
critical_code_review_comments: PurchaseModule.jsx line 692: {isOwner(user) && ( ... )} - user may be null during initial render
AuthContext.jsx uses async fetchUser() but component renders immediately without waiting
OwnerEditButton.jsx has DUPLICATE isOwner check (both parent and child check user role)
Backend API role check works correctly - user.role is 'owner' in JWT payload

success_rate.frontend: FAIL - Owner Edit Button not rendered (0/1 feature broken)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend P1 APIs all working (calculate-pricing, check-promotions, price-for-customer, owner-edit). Frontend issue: Owner Edit button not rendering in PurchaseModule.jsx because user is null during first render. Fix needed in PurchaseModule.jsx line 692 or AuthContext loading state.

rca_of_issue.issue: Owner Edit Button not visible in Purchase Orders list
rca_of_issue.root_cause: Race condition between async user fetch and component render
reproduction_steps: 3. Check AKSI column in table - NO amber/yellow Edit button visible
4. Only see: Detail eye icon, Submit/Cancel/Print buttons
rca_of_issue.technical_detail: PurchaseModule.jsx line 692 checks `isOwner(user)` but `user` is null because AuthContext.fetchUser() is async and hasn't completed. The table renders before user data is available from /api/auth/me.
rca_of_issue.fix_suggestion: Remove line 692 check or add conditional render that waits for user to be loaded: {user && isOwner(user) && ( or add loading check in component
